name: edit

count.description: [Number of lines for head/tail (default: 10); 1/2/3 for wc]

dry_run.description: [Show what would be done without doing it]

name: execute_command
description: Execute arbitrary shell command and return full output.

max_time.description: Max transfer time for curl commands (e.g. 30s, default: matches --timeout)

name: execute_sql_code
description: Execute the sql code.

name: fetch_url_via_curl
description: Fetch content from a URL using curl with enhanced options.

method.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.).

data.description: Request body data for POST/PUT requests.

max_redirects.description: Maximum number of redirects to follow.

retry.description: Number of retry attempts on failure.

description: Extract the content from a given URL using Jina AI Reader.

name: fetch_url_via_wget

method.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.).

body_data.description: Request body data for POST/PUT requests.

header.description: Additional headers (can be specified multiple times).

max_redirect.description: Maximum number of redirects to follow.

name: fs_cat
description:
Read the contents of a file at the specified path.
Use this when you need to examine the contents of an existing file.

path.description: The path of the file to read

name: fs_checksum
description: Calculate SHA256 checksum of a file.

name: fs_compress
description: Compress a file or folder into a tar.gz archive.

name: fs_extract
description: Extract a tar.gz or zip archive.

name: fs_find_large
description: Find large files in a directory.

min_size.description: The minimum size of files to find.

name: fs_ls
description: List all files and directories at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the directory to list

name: fs_mime_type
description: Identify the MIME type of a file.

name: fs_mkdir
description: Create a new directory at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the directory to create

name: fs_patch
description:
Apply a patch to a file at the specified path.
This can be used to edit the file, without having to rewrite the whole file.

path.description: The path of the file to apply to

contents.description:
The patch to apply to the file

Here is an example of a patch block that can be applied to modify the file to request the user's name:
--- a/hello.py
+++ b/hello.py
@@ ... @@
 def hello():
-    print("Hello World")
+    name = input("What is your name? ")
+    print(f"Hello {name}")

name: fs_rm
description: Remove the file or directory at the specified path.

path.description: The path of the file or directory to remove

name: fs_write
description: Write, append, insert, or replace content in a file

path.description: The path of the file to write to

contents.description: The contents to write/append/replace. Use '-' to read from stdin

append.description: Append the contents to the end of the file instead of overwriting

line.description: The specific line number to replace with the contents

insert.description: Insert the contents *before* a specific line number

backup.description: Create a backup of the file before modifying

parents.description: Create parent directories if they don't exist

match.description: Regex pattern to perform global replacement on the file content

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for file operations

no_trailing_newline.description: Do not add trailing newline to content

format.description: Format file after operation (none, json, xml, yaml)

in_place.description: Edit file in place without temporary file

context.description: Show context lines around changes (number of lines)

follow_symlinks.description: Follow symlinks instead of replacing them

Search Wikipedia for a query.

Get an answer to a question using Wolfram Alpha. Input should the query in English.
Use it to answer user questions that require computation, detailed facts, data analysis, or complex queries.
